user_query:
I save new recipes as plain text files under input/recipes/. Please create a small helper that watches that folder and, whenever a new .txt recipe appears (or on first start with existing files), it parses the file, rewrites the description to be clearer and shorter, updates an index, computes simple stats, and writes a short family digest message.

Details and constraints:
- Watch input/recipes/ for *.txt. On startup, treat any existing .txt files as newly added and process them, then continue watching for future additions/changes.
- Each recipe file uses labeled sections (Title, Category, Prep Time, Cook Time, Servings, Description, Ingredients, Directions) like the provided examples. Parse at minimum: Title, Category, Prep Time (minutes), Cook Time (minutes), Servings (integer), Description (free text), Ingredients (bullet lines starting with "-").
- Rewrite step (required): From Description, create a ConciseDescription by removing these filler words if present: [really, super, actually, just, basically]; then trim to a maximum of 180 characters without cutting a word in half. Keep it friendly and clear. Use this ConciseDescription in outputs below.
- Structured index (required): Update output/recipes_index.csv with one row per unique recipe file containing columns: file_name, title, category, prep_minutes, cook_minutes, total_minutes (prep+cook), servings, ingredients_count (number of ingredient bullet lines), concise_description. Create the file if it doesn’t exist; update a row if the source file content changes.
- Aggregate stats (required): Write output/stats.json after each event with fields: recipe_count, avg_prep_minutes, avg_total_minutes, counts_by_category (object of category->count), unique_ingredients_count. For unique_ingredients_count, normalize each ingredient name as the text before the first parenthesis, lowercased and trimmed; count distinct values across all processed recipes. Round averages to 1 decimal.
- Digest message: For each event that processes one or more new recipes, write output/messages/new_recipe_digest.txt with this structure:
  1) First line: "New recipes added: N" (N = number of newly processed recipes in this event)
  2) Then one line per new recipe: "- {Title} — {ConciseDescription}"
  3) Final line: "Top category: {category_with_highest_count_overall} | Avg total time: {overall_avg_total_minutes_rounded_0} min"
  Overwrite this file each event.
- Idempotency/state: Maintain output/state/processed_files.json to track processed files with their base file name and a content hash. Use it to avoid duplicate rows for the same file and to detect updates when the hash changes.

Please implement this so I can run it locally and see the files under output/ update as I add or change recipes in input/recipes/. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
